jokes.text: See you in Hell, document.
jokes.setup: Family celebrating paying off mortgage - should be grateful/relieved
jokes.punchline: Homer dramatically condemns the mortgage document to Hell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - takes normal relief and turns it into dramatic vengeance. Economic and character-specific.

jokes.text: A roll, sir? - Screw you.
jokes.setup: Homer advising family to eat smart at buffet, go for expensive items
jokes.punchline: Immediately and rudely rejects cheap bread roll
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's commitment to his buffet strategy through immediate rudeness to server.

jokes.text: I win. Thinner... thinner... too thin. Better luck next time, 'Tommy-- Virginia Beach.'
jokes.setup: Someone slicing meat very thin at carving station
jokes.punchline: Goes too far and ruins it, casually dismisses customer with full name and location
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity of addressing customer by full name and location adds unexpected detail.

jokes.text: You ate meat. You ate meat. You ate fruit. You ate fruit.
jokes.setup: Kids arguing at fancy brunch
jokes.punchline: Simplistic taunting about each other's food choices
jokes.explanation: Kid-like argument fits characters but fairly basic humor.

jokes.text: What do I have to do to enjoy a Sunday brunch with my secret other family?
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about kids embarrassing him at brunch
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions having a 'secret other family'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual delivery of shocking information. Classic Simpsons dark humor with perfect economy.

jokes.text: And the worst part is, this is brunch, so you've ruined two meals.
jokes.setup: Homer upset about kids ruining brunch experience
jokes.punchline: Focuses on practical concern that brunch counts as two meals
jokes.explanation: Homer's food-focused priorities shine through - escalates complaint in character-specific way.

jokes.text: I'll see you all at lupper.
jokes.setup: Homer storms off after brunch disaster
jokes.punchline: Invents meal name 'lupper' (lunch+supper)
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau that sounds plausible. Good wordplay execution.

jokes.text: Why did I ever have kids? I could have written symphonies, or been Shakespeare.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting his life choices
jokes.punchline: Homer Simpson claiming he could have been Shakespeare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion. The contrast between his actual capabilities and Shakespeare is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, who are you, the health inspector? Yes.
jokes.setup: Moe sarcastically asks if customer is health inspector
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact confirmation that he actually is
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline structure with perfect timing. Simple but effective misdirection.

jokes.text: But then where would I go to get away from my wife?
jokes.setup: Health inspector threatens to close down filthy bar
jokes.punchline: Reveals he needs the dive bar for personal escape
jokes.explanation: Shows even authority figures have personal motivations for keeping dive bars open.

jokes.text: Lenny, you don't have to hide that rat. Off you go, little fella.
jokes.setup: Health inspector doing 'inspection' of obviously unsanitary bar
jokes.punchline: Casually dismisses rat instead of citing violation
jokes.explanation: Shows corrupt inspector's casual attitude toward health violations.

jokes.text: My pop would buy me a malted for every tooth of Moe's I knocked out. That time you blinded me, he gave you a bike.
jokes.setup: Explaining childhood friendship through fathers making them fight
jokes.punchline: Escalates from tooth-loss rewards to bike for blinding
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation that explains their friendship through childhood trauma.

jokes.text: That sure was a good-soundin' bike...
jokes.setup: Learning friend got bike for permanently injuring him as child
jokes.punchline: Moe's wistful appreciation for the quality of bike he never got
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe response - focuses on material object rather than injustice of situation.

jokes.text: Oh my God, he's dead. Okay, which one of you guys parked in front of the hydrant?
jokes.setup: Health inspector drops dead after eating bar food
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to unrelated parking violation concern
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - priorities completely wrong for situation. Shows bar patrons' twisted perspective.

jokes.text: Uh, look, I didn't see nothin' here, okay? Just a bunch of innocent guys, sitting around, none of them dead.
jokes.setup: Trying to cover up health inspector's death
jokes.punchline: Explicitly states 'none of them dead' while covering up death
jokes.explanation: Obvious cover-up made worse by explicitly mentioning what they're denying.

jokes.text: Uh, well, ya see, trash day ain't 'til Wednesday, so...
jokes.setup: New health inspector points out predecessor's body still on floor
jokes.punchline: Moe treats corpse as regular trash disposal issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe logic - reduces human corpse to mundane waste management problem.

jokes.text: Chicken skins in soap dispenser... cigarette butts in the air... toilet on the roof...
jokes.setup: Listing health code violations at Moe's
jokes.punchline: Escalates from gross to impossible (toilet on roof)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure building to absurd architectural impossibility.

jokes.text: 'M' is for Moe, the owner of Moe's. 'O' is for the 'O' in the middle of Moe's.
jokes.setup: Giving tribute speech using acronym format for 'MOE'
jokes.punchline: Lazy/circular definition for O
jokes.explanation: Shows character's limited creativity when trying to be meaningful.

jokes.text: 'E' is for acceptance. The feeling I always got here at Moe's.
jokes.setup: Continuing MOE acronym tribute
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly heartfelt and genuine moment for dive bar
jokes.explanation: Unexpected sincerity from barfly creates sweet moment amid the absurdity.

jokes.text: I can't go to a gay bar-- I'm too fat.
jokes.setup: Looking for alternative bars since Moe's is closed
jokes.punchline: Barney's bizarre reasoning about weight as gay bar barrier
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney non-sequitur logic about social situations.

jokes.text: No one's ever trusted me before-- except for that one guy who shouldn't have. That was me. Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Moe grateful for Homer's trust with loan
jokes.punchline: Homer admits he was the guy who shouldn't have trusted Moe
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that reveals Homer's awareness of his own poor judgment.

jokes.text: Marge! How dare you open a letter addressed to both of us!
jokes.setup: Marge discovers Homer got new mortgage without telling her
jokes.punchline: Homer acts offended about letter-opening when he's the guilty one
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer deflection - creates fake outrage to avoid real issue.

jokes.text: That's because I hate her.
jokes.setup: Marge says she'd like Homer to come to her mother's, he never does
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt honesty about hating mother-in-law
jokes.explanation: Homer's brutal honesty delivered matter-of-factly. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: Nor a wife, a friend, a chum, a casual acquaintance, a pen pal, a parrot, a meaningful conversation, a brief hug or eye contact.
jokes.setup: Moe saying he's never had a partner
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly basic human connections he lacks
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that perfectly captures Moe's profound isolation. Each item gets more pathetic.

jokes.text: And they've blocked my number.
jokes.setup: Moe about to call suicide hotline
jokes.punchline: Even suicide prevention service has blocked him
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant - the ultimate rejection. Even people whose job is helping suicidal people won't talk to him.

jokes.text: How many cigars are they allowed to have? Bart sleeps in the microwave, right?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to avoid childcare responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd questions about basic childcare
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's complete disconnect from parenting reality through escalating questions.

jokes.text: How many magic beans should I sell the baby for? Three? That's me, jerk-ass Homer.
jokes.setup: Continuing absurd childcare questions
jokes.punchline: References fairy tale logic then identifies himself as 'jerk-ass Homer'
jokes.explanation: Self-aware moment where Homer acknowledges his own incompetence.

jokes.text: Well... as fabulous as your regulars are, a remodel might bring in a higher class of lush.
jokes.setup: Marge diplomatically suggesting bar improvements
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment followed by goal of attracting 'higher class of lush'
jokes.explanation: Marge's polite way of saying the clientele are terrible drunks.

jokes.text: And I ain't changin' it for any dame, skirt, Susie-Q or face-macer.
jokes.setup: Moe resistant to Marge's changes
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly bizarre terms for women ending with 'face-macer'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe terminology - escalates from dated to nonsensical.

jokes.text: Hey, hold the phone. An English pub-- that just might work. In song... My bar could be British Instead of arm-pittish, so why don't we all... Eh, screw it.
jokes.setup: Moe getting caught up in musical number
jokes.punchline: Abruptly abandons song mid-verse
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke where character gets tired of the musical format mid-song.

jokes.text: My first credit card. Wow, the numbers are all bumpy-like.
jokes.setup: Barney getting his first credit card
jokes.punchline: Fascinated by basic embossed numbers
jokes.explanation: Shows Barney's childlike wonder at basic technology/financial instruments.

jokes.text: Marge Simpson and Moe Szyslak, here's a mash note to your bangers-- I wish you could live in me forever.
jokes.setup: Food critic reviewing the British pub food
jokes.punchline: Creepy/sexual phrasing about food living inside him
jokes.explanation: Awkward food criticism with unintentionally sexual undertones.

jokes.text: Just what are you inferring? I'm not inferring anything. You infer. I imply. Whew, that's a relief.
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting something about Marge and Moe
jokes.punchline: Homer corrects grammar then is relieved by technicality
jokes.explanation: Homer's selective intelligence about grammar lets him avoid dealing with real issue.

jokes.text: Ah, yes, Detroit-style. Very nice.
jokes.setup: Marge demonstrating 'bum's rush' technique
jokes.punchline: Moe categorizing it by city style
jokes.explanation: Moe's expertise in violence techniques shown through casual classification.

jokes.text: You and I are... marriage friends.
jokes.setup: Comparing her relationships with Moe vs Homer
jokes.punchline: Reduces marriage to just being 'marriage friends'
jokes.explanation: Marge's diplomatic way of downplaying marital relationship.

jokes.text: Cards! 52 jokers playing the game of their lives.
jokes.setup: Parody movie trailer for anthropomorphic cards
jokes.punchline: Describes deck as '52 jokers' (impossible deck composition)
jokes.explanation: Movie trailer parody with basic card deck logic error for absurd effect.

jokes.text: Hey, Jack, you got any twos? You can't handle the twos!
jokes.setup: Card movie continuing
jokes.punchline: A Few Good Men reference with card game
jokes.explanation: Good movie reference adaptation. 'You can't handle the twos' works well as parody but relies on existing quote.

jokes.text: Oh, Marge told me. We share everything. You do?
jokes.setup: Moe knows personal details about Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization of intimate information sharing
jokes.explanation: Good relationship tension. Homer's slow realization about information sharing creates appropriate discomfort.

jokes.text: Can I learn it at a happy place?
jokes.setup: Being told he needs to learn sad truth about emotional affairs
jokes.punchline: Homer's only concern is the location's mood
jokes.explanation: Homer avoids serious emotional content by focusing on setting preferences.

jokes.text: Ooh, get her a Life Magazine from the week she was born. I'll always treasure mine.
jokes.setup: Barney giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific magazine gift suggestion with personal attachment
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment from Barney. The specific gift idea and his treasuring his own copy is endearing.

jokes.text: Cuckold! Cuckold! Cuckold! What's a cuckold?
jokes.setup: Crowd chanting 'cuckold' at Homer
jokes.punchline: Child asking what the word means while participating
jokes.explanation: Child innocently participating in adult mockery they don't understand.

jokes.text: Safety instructions are two dollars. If you require wheelchair assistance, you picked the wrong airline.
jokes.setup: No Frills Airlines introduction
jokes.punchline: Charging for safety info and refusing accessibility
jokes.explanation: Good airline parody. The escalation from paid safety to refusing disabled passengers is dark but effective.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm just excited because they're showing an episode of Boy Meets World on this flight. Oh no, wait. That's only on flights from Europe. We just get that little plane.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining why his lip is trembling
jokes.punchline: Deflects with increasingly specific airline trivia
jokes.explanation: Moe's nervous deflection through oddly specific airline entertainment knowledge.

jokes.text: Simpson, you were going a hundred in a 25 zone, and you're not gonna flirt your way outta this one.
jokes.setup: Police stopping Homer for speeding
jokes.punchline: Implies Homer usually flirts his way out of tickets
jokes.explanation: Suggests Homer has history of inappropriate ticket-avoidance behavior.

jokes.text: Really? Well, why didn't you say so? Let's roll! You didn't work this hard to save my marriage.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to get to airport to save marriage
jokes.punchline: Wiggum helps with chase, Lou points out hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Lou calling out Wiggum's marriage failures. Self-aware police humor.

jokes.text: Time for some shuba-duba in Aruba.
jokes.setup: Arriving in Aruba for romantic getaway
jokes.punchline: Moe's crude rhyming euphemism
jokes.explanation: Typical crude Moe wordplay with location name.

jokes.text: That sounds like Homer. No it isn't. I don't hear nothin'. You're crazy. Get outta here. Will you marry me?
jokes.setup: Homer's voice heard outside plane
jokes.punchline: Moe's rapid denial escalating to marriage proposal
jokes.explanation: Good panic escalation from Moe. His rapid progression from denial to proposal shows his desperation.

jokes.text: Homer! How'd you get in here? Toilet hole.
jokes.setup: Homer surprising them in airplane
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of impossible entry method
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and Homer's casual attitude toward impossible physical feat.

jokes.text: What's her favorite food? Ice? Wrong! It's buttered noodles. He's right.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming to know Marge better than Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd guess of 'ice' as food, then correct answer
jokes.explanation: Homer's completely wrong guess followed by surprisingly specific correct knowledge.

jokes.text: At least I had a couple of sips from your Coke when you went to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Moe rejected by Marge after trying to steal her
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small consolation prize he took
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment. Finding consolation in secretly drinking someone's Coke is perfectly pathetic and character-specific.

jokes.text: At least I had a couple of sips from your Coke when you went to the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Moe accepting romantic rejection from Marge
jokes.punchline: Finding small consolation in creepy beverage theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - finding pathetic victory in creepy behavior.

jokes.text: You're my Homie-womie-romie-domie. And you're my Margie-wargie-bargie fargie-gargie-margie-targie-largie.
jokes.setup: Romantic reconciliation with pet names
jokes.punchline: Homer's escalating nonsense rhyme gets completely out of hand
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to stop rhyming once he starts. Gets repetitive but fits character.

jokes.text: I may have dodged a bullet here.
jokes.setup: Watching Homer and Marge's overly cutesy interaction
jokes.punchline: Moe realizing he's better off not being with Marge
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Moe's practical realization. Nice economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: Know-it-all bitch.
jokes.setup: Moe just thanked Marge for kind words about his romantic potential
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults her under his breath
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character beat - can't help being awful even when receiving kindness.

jokes.text: Listen, I got to tell you, I chew in my sleep. Yeah, well, I sweat blood.
jokes.setup: Sharing sleeping habits as roommates
jokes.punchline: Moe's response escalates to impossible/disturbing condition
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from odd to medically impossible. Classic one-upmanship.

jokes.text: Who's watching the kids? Are you sure Mom and Dad want us to enter a European balloon race? Sure. Why not?
jokes.setup: Marge suddenly remembering about children
jokes.punchline: Kids have started dangerous activity while unsupervised
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons neglected children subplot with absurd escalation.
